subject: Raylawni G. Branch and Jeanette Smith

pending_items.correction: Coffeyville (MS, near Yazoo) or "Hot Coffee" (Covington County MS)
pending_items.notes: "she ran off from it's a little area out near Prenniss, Coffinchett"; uncertain — both candidates are real small MS communities near Prentiss; could also be "Coffeeville" (MS) // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"Frenchmen out of originally out of Newfoundland and they... and the Native Americans"; speaker means Cajuns (Acadians from Nova Scotia, not Newfoundland — possibly speaker's own historical conflation, not a Whisper error) // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"Mr. Damon was before Mr. Fairly" — confirmed via context as Dahmer (NAACP president before JC Fairley); also "Mr. Damon was one of them" (referring to Shady Grove Baptist Church congregation, which is canonical Vernon Dahmer's church) // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]
"Mr. Damer died January 10th, 1966"; canonical Vernon Dahmer's assassination by Klan firebombing // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]
Same canonical date "January 10, 1966" — confirmed identification // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"they tried to talk him out of coming out here each time that last time he came, he came alone... they tried to talk Mr. Knauer out of them?"; same person, alternate Whisper rendering // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"Mr. Canard had come to Hadithworth to help his mother"; same person; recurring throughout the transcript // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending] // [LAYER-5: D3-catalog-contradiction (catalog 'E': 'Clyde Kennard (MS Southern College pre-Meredith integration martyr)'), ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"They would meet at Smith-Trucks' door"; "Smith in the beginning. Smith-Juckstown"; canonical Smith Drug Store at Mobile and Sixth in Hattiesburg, frequent NAACP-meeting site // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"Mr. Berger, who was the Black principal, if I have to identify people, Black or White, who was the Black principal over in Rowan"; speaker's identification of the Rowan High School principal; need to verify name with Hattiesburg school records // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"they had an organization among themselves"; speaker references unnamed MS Black-doctor network (Dr. Mason, Dr. Smith) without further detail; could correspond to Drs. Aaron Shirley, James Anderson, James Mason — canonical Medical Committee for Human Rights MS chapter doctors // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]
Hattiesburg-area organizer; speaker's circle; treat as speaker's actual recollection // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"Far General Hospital, it was called Methodist Hospital. It's now merit-wessling out on 98"; canonical Forrest General Hospital (now Merit Health Wesley) // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

pending_items.correction: Pearson v. Murray (1936) / Missouri ex rel. Gaines v. Canada (1938) — out-of-state tuition equalization
pending_items.notes: "if there was something that you wanted to do that wasn't taught in Mississippi, you could be sitting out of the state in the state with Peyton DeVille"; the canonical "out-of-state tuition" segregation evasion — sending Black students out-of-state for graduate/professional school to avoid integrating in-state grad schools; "Peyton DeVille" is Whisper-garbled; possible candidates: "paying tuition" or a specific case-name reference // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]

"Mrs. James had prepared us well on how on the Constitution... Mrs. Marjorie James with our world history teacher"; Rowan High School teacher; identification // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]
"they later, I think, they still sent him on parchment"; canonical Parchman Farm where Clyde Kennard was sent after the 1962 frame-up trial // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]
Hattiesburg-summer cooking-organizer; speaker's circle // [LAYER-5: D2-ambiguous, ensemble-adjudication-pending]
